llm_reasoning: Suitability for corn is poor, primarily due to the extremely low mean temperature (5.572°C), which is insufficient for corn's heat requirements and growing degree days, severely limiting its ability to mature. The strongest positive factors are the ideal soil pH (6.52) and good organic carbon content (17.98 g/kg), which are excellent for corn. Operational risks include insufficient heat units for maturity and potential late/early frosts. Mitigation involves selecting very early-maturing, cold-tolerant varieties or considering alternative crops. Good drainage is also crucial given high rainfall and clay content.
